title: CSA CCM v4 to GCP Security Services Cross-Reference
description: Maps CSA Cloud Controls Matrix v4 domains to Google Cloud Platform (GCP) security services and features that implement or support each domain's control objectives. Use to identify which GCP services satisfy CCM requirements for cloud security assessments.
verificationNote: GCP service mappings are based on publicly documented service capabilities as of 2025. GCP services evolve frequently -- verify current capabilities against Google Cloud documentation. Not endorsed by CSA or Google.

gcpServices.description: Compliance enforcement for regulated workloads with automated controls for FedRAMP, HIPAA, PCI DSS. Supports A&A-01 (audit planning) and A&A-05 (regulatory mapping).
Access to GCP compliance reports (SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS). Supports A&A-05.
Automated IAM policy analysis for compliance. Supports A&A-03 (risk-based planning).
Web Application Firewall with pre-configured WAF rules, DDoS protection, and bot management. Implements AIS-01 (application security) for web applications.
Full-lifecycle API management with security policies, OAuth 2.0, rate limiting, and threat protection. Supports AIS-04 (secure application design).
Automated vulnerability scanner for App Engine, GKE, and Compute Engine web applications. Implements AIS-02 (application security testing).
CI/CD with integrated security scanning and binary authorisation. Supports AIS-02.
Geo-redundant object storage with automatic failover. Supports BCR-01 (business continuity planning).
Point-in-time disk snapshots with cross-region replication. Implements BCR-04 (backup and recovery).
Automated database backups with cross-region replication. Supports BCR-01 and BCR-04.
Multi-cluster Kubernetes for cross-region application resilience. Supports BCR-02.

gcpServices.service: Retention Policy and Bucket Lock (Cloud Storage)
gcpServices.description: WORM-compliant immutable storage for backup protection. Implements BCR-04 (backup integrity).

gcpServices.service: Config Connector / Deployment Manager / Terraform on GCP
gcpServices.description: Infrastructure as Code for controlled, repeatable GCP deployments. Implements CCC-01 (change management).
Continuous configuration assessment against GCP best practices. Implements CCC-03 (configuration management).
Automated OS configuration and compliance management for Compute Engine. Supports CCC-03.
Curated catalogue of approved GCP solutions. Supports CCC-02 (quality testing) through pre-approved configurations.
Customer-managed encryption key management with software, HSM, and external key options. Implements EKM-01 through EKM-04.
FIPS 140-2 Level 3 managed HSM for cryptographic key storage. Implements EKM-02 for highest assurance.
Encryption with externally managed keys (key never leaves customer control). Implements EKM-02 for maximum key sovereignty.
Private CA for TLS certificate management. Supports DSP-02 (encryption in transit).
All GCP data encrypted at rest by default with Google-managed or CMEK. Implements DSP-01.

Sensitive data discovery, classification, de-identification, and inspection. Detects PII, financial data, credentials across GCP services. Implements DSP-01 (data classification) and DSP-02 (data protection).
Fine-grained access control at column level in BigQuery. Supports DSP-05 (data access control).
Security perimeter for GCP services preventing data exfiltration. Implements DSP-04 (data access restriction) and DSP-06 (data residency).
Restrict resource creation to approved GCP regions. Implements DSP-06 (data residency).
Automated data lifecycle management. Implements DSP-17 (data retention and deletion).
Hierarchical governance with organisation, folders, and projects. Policy inheritance. Implements GRC-01 (governance programme).
Centralised policy constraints for the entire GCP organisation. Implements GRC-01 and GRC-03 (policy enforcement).
Centralised security and compliance dashboard with posture management. Supports GRC-02 (risk management).
Zero-trust access for workforce with device trust and context-aware policies. Supports HRS security awareness through enforced access controls.
Investigation tool for user activity analysis. Supports HRS-09 (personnel security monitoring).
Core identity and access management. Roles, policies, conditions, deny policies. Implements IAM-01 and IAM-04.
Identity management with MFA, SSO, and directory services. Implements IAM-02 and IAM-03.
ML-based recommendations for removing excess permissions. Implements IAM-06 (access reviews) and IAM-09 (least privilege).
Managed identity for workloads eliminating service account key management. Implements IAM-10.
Zero-trust access framework with device trust and context-aware access. Implements IAM-12 (risk-based access control).
Admin activity, data access, system event, and policy denied logging. Supports IAM-12 and LOG-01.
Hybrid and multi-cloud Kubernetes platform. Supports IPY through portable container orchestration.
Serverless container platform compatible with Knative standard. Supports IPY through standard container interfaces.
Migration tools for moving workloads to containers. Supports portability.
Virtual network with subnets, firewall rules, and private Google access. Implements IVS-01 and IVS-06.
DDoS protection, WAF, and adaptive protection using ML. Implements IVS-04.
Hierarchical firewall policies with threat intelligence integration. Implements IVS-01 and IVS-04.
Threat detection for VMs, containers, and GCP services. Implements IVS-09.
Private connectivity to GCP services without public internet. Implements IVS-06 (network segmentation).
Memory encryption for VMs using AMD SEV. Supports IVS-09 (compute isolation) for sensitive workloads.
Comprehensive audit logging for all GCP services (admin, data access, system events). Implements LOG-01.
Centralised log management with ingestion, storage, and analysis. Implements LOG-02 (log centralisation).
Cloud-native SIEM with petabyte-scale log analysis and YARA-L detection rules. Implements LOG-03 and LOG-13.
Network traffic logging for VPC subnets. Implements LOG-01 for network visibility.
Immutable log storage with retention lock. Implements LOG-05 (log integrity and retention).
Metrics, dashboards, and alerting policies. Implements LOG-13 (alerting).

mappings.ccmDomainName: Security Incident Management, E-Discovery, & Cloud Forensics

gcpServices.description: Security orchestration and automated response with playbooks. Implements SEF-01 and SEF-02.
Centralised security finding management with severity, state tracking, and mute rules. Supports SEF-01.

gcpServices.service: Persistent Disk Snapshots for forensic preservation
gcpServices.description: Disk snapshots for forensic evidence preservation and analysis. Supports cloud forensics.
Audit log export to Cloud Storage with retention lock for forensic evidence preservation. Supports SEF.
Access to GCP compliance documentation (SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS). Supports STA-05.
Private container and package registry with vulnerability scanning. Supports STA-06 (supply chain security).
Deploy-time enforcement of container image provenance and signatures. Implements STA-06 and STA-14 (supply chain integrity).
End-to-end software supply chain security from source to deployment. Implements STA-06 comprehensively.
Vetted third-party software and services. Supports STA-01 (vendor assessment).
Continuous vulnerability assessment for GCP resources with prioritised findings. Implements TVM-01 and TVM-02.
Automated container image vulnerability scanning. Implements TVM-04.
Automated OS patching for Compute Engine instances. Implements TVM-01 (vulnerability remediation).
Threat intelligence feeds and adversary tracking. Implements TVM-06 (threat intelligence).
Automated web application vulnerability scanning. Implements TVM-02.
Zero-trust access with device trust, context-aware access, and endpoint verification. Implements UEM controls through device-based access policies.
Mobile device and endpoint management with compliance policies. Supports UEM for Google-managed endpoints.
Managed browser with security policies, extension controls, and data loss prevention. Supports UEM for browser-based cloud access.
